title: Ctenitis aquilina
taxonomy: Ctenitis aquilina is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is a species of fern in the genus Ctenitis. Ctenitis aquilina is native to Asia and is known for its distinctive frond structure. The classification of Ctenitis aquilina is further categorized under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. It is often found in shaded, humid environments and is valued for its ornamental and medicinal properties.
uses: Ctenitis aquilina has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens and reduce inflammation. In traditional medicine, Ctenitis aquilina is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Ctenitis aquilina. Some studies suggest that its extracts may have therapeutic benefits for conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

parts: Ctenitis aquilina is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. The leaves and roots of Ctenitis aquilina are considered the medicinal parts, used to treat various health issues. The leaves are often used to reduce fever and inflammation, while the roots are used to treat digestive problems. intro: Ctenitis aquilina, also known as Japanese fern or golden shield fern, is a species of plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis aquilina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenitis aquilina is often made into tea or dried powder for the treatment of respiratory and digestive problems, as well as for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
